title: lycopersicon esculentum var. leptophyllum
intro: Lycopersicon esculentum var. leptophyllum, also known as the wild tomato, is a variety of the common tomato plant that grows in the wild and has smaller fruit compared to its cultivated counterpart. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects due to its high content of lycopene and other carotenoids. The health benefits of Lycopersicon esculentum var. leptophyllum include reducing the risk of certain cancers, heart disease, and age-related macular degeneration, as well as improving overall immune function and skin health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, or by using the fruit to make a juice or tincture.
